mnemonics.title: PUMP
mnemonics.content: P – Pumping length p exists for every regular language. U – Universal condition: any string longer than p can be split. M – Middle segment y must be non‑empty. P – Pumped string xy^i z stays in the language for all i≥0.

mnemonics.title: XYZ Decomposition
mnemonics.content: Remember s = xyz: X = prefix (|xy| ≤ p), Y = the pumpable middle (|y|>0), Z = suffix. Repeating Y (i times) keeps the string in L.

mnemonics.title: KLEENE STAR (U*C)
mnemonics.content: Kleene’s theorem says any regular language can be built using Union (U), Concatenation (·), and Star (Kleene *). Think “U‑C‑Star” as the three ops.

mnemonics.title: K‑R‑E = Regular Expressions
mnemonics.content: K – Kleene, R – Regular, E – Expression. The theorem states: RE ⇔ FA (finite automaton).

mnemonics.title: P‑U‑M‑P‑L‑E‑M‑A
mnemonics.content: P‑owerful, U‑seful, M‑ethod, P‑roving, L‑anguage, E‑xactly, M‑atches, A‑utomata: the pumping lemma is a powerful tool to prove a language is not regular.

acronyms.usage: A generic model (DFA or NFA) that recognises regular languages.
A FA where each state has exactly one transition per input symbol; used in proving regularity.
A FA that may have multiple possible transitions; easier to construct from REs.
A pattern describing a regular language using union, concatenation, and Kleene star.
A language recognised by a finite automaton (DFA/NFA).
Denotes zero or more repetitions of a language (or expression).
One of the three operations (union, concatenation, star) building REs.
The operation of joining two languages/expressions in sequence.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a long string entering a DFA. Because the DFA has only p states, the first p symbols must repeat a state (pigeonhole principle). The loop between the first repeated state and itself corresponds to the y segment; you can travel around the loop any number of times, i.e., pump y.
Think of a directed edge that returns to its start (a self‑loop) labelled with the sub‑expression. Traversing that edge zero or more times corresponds to the star operator.

Build a small NFA fragment for each basic RE (single symbol, ε, empty set) and then compose them using three construction patterns: one for union (two NFAs side by side), one for concatenation (connect end of first to start of second), and one for star (add a loop and epsilon transitions).

A DFA is a directed graph where each node is a state and each edge labelled by an input symbol. Regular languages correspond to paths that start at the start state and end in a final (accepting) state.

analogies.analogy: Think of a bike tire: once you pump it enough (reach length p), any additional pump (adding a repeated y segment) keeps the tire inflated (the string stays in the language). The pumping action is repeatable, but the tire still works.
An all‑you‑can‑eat buffet: you may take zero plates (ε), one plate (concatenation), or go back for unlimited additional plates – the star means 'any number of repetitions including zero'.

A recipe (regular expression) tells you how to combine ingredients (symbols) using steps like mixing (union), stacking (concatenation), and stirring repeatedly (star). The kitchen (finite automaton) actually carries out the recipe to produce a dish (accept/reject a string).
